title: Python generated CLI completion
larger_intended_outcome: Complete the Python side of #892 by moving command parser, dispatch, interface behavior, adapter metadata, and conformance expectations behind implementation-independent contracts or IR, leaving cli.py files as thin runtime primitive adapters only.
non_goals: Do not claim weak-agent-safe adapter maturity is full Python generated CLI completion.
Do not move live workspace inspection, mutation orchestration, or package-specific runtime primitive implementation into generated code.
Do not advance Bash, PowerShell, or TypeScript in this Python lane except where shared contracts must remain implementation-independent.

candidate_lanes.title: Python runtime handler boundary
candidate_lanes.outcome: Generated Python dispatch can identify supported operation ids from IR while cli.py exposes only thin operation handler adapters for hand-owned runtime primitives.
candidate_lanes.proof: Generated package static proof plus Python conformance prove operation dispatch is generated and runtime handlers remain thin package-local adapters.

candidate_lanes.title: Python handwritten parser retirement for generated commands
candidate_lanes.outcome: Commands covered by command_package_ir no longer require duplicate handwritten argparse definitions in package cli.py files.
candidate_lanes.proof: Static proof rejects duplicated handwritten parser ownership for generated command/interface surfaces; Python black-box conformance still passes.

candidate_lanes.title: Python command/interface change gate
candidate_lanes.outcome: Future package command/interface changes are forced through contracts, IR, generators, regenerated outputs, and black-box conformance rather than package-local executable cli.py edits.
candidate_lanes.proof: Proof selection and static generated-package checks fail when command/interface changes bypass contract or generated surfaces.

candidate_lanes.title: Python generated CLI completion promotion
candidate_lanes.outcome: The command-package IR can promote Python from adapter-layer-proven-not-full-generated-cli to full generated CLI completion only after parser, dispatch, interface, proof, and runtime-handler boundaries are all proven.
candidate_lanes.proof: Generated package static proof, Python Docker conformance, root CLI authority, closeout trust, and package planning summary all agree that original Python generated-CLI intent is satisfied.

dependency_assumptions: Runtime primitive behavior remains hand-owned and can be called from thin operation handlers.
Generated Python parser and dispatch should consume command_package_ir and conformance contracts as the single source of truth.
Each lane must keep Python work independent from Bash and PowerShell transport promotion.
parallelization_assumptions: The first two Python lanes are ordered because parser retirement depends on a clearer runtime-handler boundary.
Interface-change gating can be explored in parallel only after the parser retirement boundary is explicit.
proof_expectations: Each promoted Python lane must include Python black-box conformance and Docker-backed Python conformance unless the lane is planning-only.
Closeout must run or inspect closeout_trust before claiming Python completion.
The command-package IR python_cli_completion.current_state must stay adapter-layer-proven-not-full-generated-cli until the final promotion lane is proven.
promotion_rule: Promote only the next ready Python lane with clear generated-output ownership, no Bash/PowerShell scope, and proof that implementation-independent artifacts remain authoritative.
notes: Created after #921 showed that adapter maturity was incorrectly treated as Python generated-CLI completion. Runtime handler boundary and handwritten parser retirement have been promoted and implemented on codex/finish-python-generated-cli; remaining Python work starts at the interface-change gate.
